INSERT INTO dim_customer (sk, customer_id, name, email, start_date, end_date, is_current)
SELECT dim_cust_seq.NEXTVAL, src.customer_id, src.name, src.email, SYSDATE, DATE '9999-12-31', 'Y'
FROM   staging_customers src
       JOIN dim_customer tgt ON src.customer_id = tgt.customer_id
WHERE  tgt.is_current = 'N' AND tgt.end_date = TRUNC(SYSDATE);

32. SQL Keyword Master List

SQL / ANSI Keywords

SELECT FROM WHERE GROUP BY HAVING ORDER BY DISTINCT
INNER LEFT RIGHT FULL OUTER JOIN ON USING NATURAL CROSS
UNION UNION ALL INTERSECT EXCEPT MINUS
INSERT INTO VALUES UPDATE SET DELETE MERGE USING
CREATE ALTER DROP TRUNCATE RENAME COMMENT
GRANT REVOKE COMMIT ROLLBACK SAVEPOINT
WITH AS RECURSIVE
CASE WHEN THEN ELSE END
IN EXISTS BETWEEN LIKE ANY ALL NOT AND OR IS NULL
OFFSET FETCH FIRST NEXT ROWS ONLY WITH TIES
PARTITION BY OVER ROWS RANGE UNBOUNDED PRECEDING FOLLOWING CURRENT ROW

Oracle Reserved Keywords

ROWNUM ROWID DUAL SYSDATE SYSTIMESTAMP USER LEVEL PRIOR
CONNECT BY START WITH NOCYCLE
DECODE NVL NVL2 NULLIF COALESCE GREATEST LEAST
PIVOT UNPIVOT LISTAGG WITHIN GROUP
FLASHBACK AS OF SCN TIMESTAMP VERSIONS BETWEEN
XMLTYPE XMLTABLE JSON_TABLE JSON_OBJECT JSON_ARRAY
BULK COLLECT FORALL EXECUTE IMMEDIATE
PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION EXCEPTION_INIT
RAISE_APPLICATION_ERROR RAISE SQLERRM SQLCODE
%TYPE %ROWTYPE %FOUND %NOTFOUND %ISOPEN %ROWCOUNT
SYS_CONTEXT ORA_ROWSCN SYS_CONNECT_BY_PATH CONNECT_BY_ROOT

Analytical Function Keywords

ROW_NUMBER RANK DENSE_RANK NTILE
LAG LEAD FIRST_VALUE LAST_VALUE NTH_VALUE
PERCENT_RANK CUME_DIST RATIO_TO_REPORT
STDDEV VARIANCE MEDIAN
LISTAGG WITHIN GROUP
OVER PARTITION BY ORDER BY ROWS RANGE BETWEEN
UNBOUNDED PRECEDING CURRENT ROW UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING

PL/SQL Keywords

DECLARE BEGIN END EXCEPTION
IF ELSIF ELSE THEN END IF
CASE WHEN THEN ELSE END CASE
LOOP WHILE FOR REVERSE EXIT CONTINUE
CURSOR OPEN FETCH INTO CLOSE FOR UPDATE OF CURRENT OF
PROCEDURE FUNCTION PACKAGE BODY RETURN IS AS
IN OUT NOCOPY DEFAULT
TYPE RECORD TABLE VARRAY INDEX BY
BULK COLLECT FORALL LIMIT INDICES OF VALUES OF
RAISE RAISE_APPLICATION_ERROR SQLERRM SQLCODE
PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION EXCEPTION_INIT SERIALLY_REUSABLE
PIPE ROW PIPELINED RESULT_CACHE DETERMINISTIC
AUTHID CURRENT_USER DEFINER

33. Cheat Sheets & Quick Reference

33.1 Window Function Quick Reference

RANKING:
ROW_NUMBER()     → 1,2,3,4,5     (no ties, always unique)
RANK()           → 1,2,2,4,5     (ties, then gap)
DENSE_RANK()     → 1,2,2,3,4     (ties, no gap)
NTILE(n)         → 1,1,2,2,3     (divide into n buckets)

NAVIGATION:
LAG(col, offset, default)        → previous row value
LEAD(col, offset, default)       → next row value
FIRST_VALUE(col)                 → first value in window
LAST_VALUE(col) [ROWS UNBOUNDED] → last value in window
NTH_VALUE(col, n)                → n-th value in window

AGGREGATE (add OVER):
SUM(), AVG(), COUNT(), MIN(), MAX()
→ Remove PARTITION BY for global window
→ Remove ORDER BY for non-cumulative aggregation
→ Add ORDER BY + ROWS frame for running totals

STATISTICAL:
PERCENT_RANK()   → (rank-1)/(rows-1), range 0–1
CUME_DIST()      → rank/rows, range (0,1]
RATIO_TO_REPORT(col) → col/SUM(col) for partition

33.2 Join Type Quick Reference

INNER JOIN:   A ∩ B       Only matching rows
LEFT JOIN:    A           All of A, NULLs where no B match
RIGHT JOIN:   B           All of B, NULLs where no A match
FULL JOIN:    A ∪ B       All rows, NULLs where no match
CROSS JOIN:   A × B       Cartesian product: |A| * |B| rows
SELF JOIN:    A JOIN A    Table joined to itself (hierarchy, adjacency)
SEMI JOIN:    EXISTS       Only A columns, exits on first B match
ANTI JOIN:    NOT EXISTS   A rows with NO match in B

33.3 PL/SQL Exception Reference

Named exceptions        Error Code
──────────────────────────────────
NO_DATA_FOUND           ORA-01403
TOO_MANY_ROWS           ORA-01422
CURSOR_ALREADY_OPEN     ORA-06511
DUP_VAL_ON_INDEX        ORA-00001
INVALID_NUMBER          ORA-01722
VALUE_ERROR             ORA-06502
ZERO_DIVIDE             ORA-01476
STORAGE_ERROR           ORA-06500
TIMEOUT_ON_RESOURCE     ORA-00051
NOT_LOGGED_ON           ORA-01012
LOGIN_DENIED            ORA-01017
OTHERS                  (catch-all)

33.4 SQL Execution Order Reference

SQL Clause Execution Order:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  1. FROM        — identify source tables
  2. JOIN        — join tables
  3. WHERE       — filter rows (before grouping)
  4. GROUP BY    — form groups
  5. HAVING      — filter groups
  6. SELECT      — compute output columns / window functions
  7. DISTINCT    — remove duplicates
  8. ORDER BY    — sort results
  9. OFFSET      — skip rows
 10. FETCH FIRST — limit rows

Consequences:
- Can't use SELECT alias in WHERE (step 3 before step 6)
- CAN use SELECT alias in ORDER BY (step 8 after step 6)
- HAVING can reference aggregate functions; WHERE cannot
- Window functions execute in step 6 — can't filter on them in WHERE
  (wrap in subquery/CTE to filter on window function result)

33.5 Oracle vs ANSI SQL Key Differences

Feature ANSI SQL Oracle
String concat `\ \ ` `\ \ orCONCAT()`
Null functions COALESCE, NULLIF + NVL, NVL2, DECODE
Top-N rows FETCH FIRST n ROWS Also ROWNUM, ROW_NUMBER
Outer join LEFT JOIN Also (+) syntax (legacy)
String type VARCHAR VARCHAR2 (use this in Oracle)
Sequence GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY SEQUENCE.NEXTVAL
Date type DATE (date only) DATE (date + time)
Hierarchical Recursive CTE CONNECT BY + Recursive CTE
Row count LIMIT n (MySQL/PG) FETCH FIRST n ROWS ONLY
Dual Not standard FROM DUAL (single-row virtual table)
Regex Standard varies REGEXP_LIKE, REGEXP_SUBSTR etc.
String agg STRING_AGG (standard) LISTAGG

33.6 Index Decision Guide

Cardinality      Volume     DML     Recommended Index
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HIGH (unique)    Any        Any     B-tree (unique or non-unique)
MEDIUM           Large      Low     B-tree
LOW (≤8 vals)   Large      Low     Bitmap (DW/reporting only)
LOW              Any        High    Avoid index or use B-tree
Composite        Large      Low     B-tree on (equality_cols, range_col)
Function result  Large      Low     Function-Based Index (FBI)
Subset of rows   Large      Any     Partial index (WHERE clause)

Common Composite Index Mistakes:
❌ (range_col, equality_col) — range col first, index less useful
✅ (equality_col, range_col) — equality first, then range
✅ (high_card_col, low_card_col) — generally more selective first
   EXCEPTION: leading column must be in WHERE for index to be used

33.7 Top Interview Questions & Model Answers

Q: Difference between WHERE and HAVING?

WHERE filters rows before grouping (cannot use aggregate functions). HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY (can use aggregate functions). Both can appear in same query.

Q: What is a correlated subquery?

A subquery that references a column from the outer query. Executed once per row of the outer query. Generally less efficient than joins but sometimes necessary. Can often be rewritten as a join or window function.

Q: Why does NOT IN fail with NULLs?

If the subquery returns any NULL value, x NOT IN (1, 2, NULL) evaluates to UNKNOWN for every x, returning zero rows. Use NOT EXISTS instead, which handles NULLs correctly.

Q: RANK vs DENSE_RANK vs ROW_NUMBER?

ROW_NUMBER: always unique sequential (1,2,3,4). RANK: gaps after ties (1,2,2,4). DENSE_RANK: no gaps after ties (1,2,2,3). Use DENSE_RANK for "top N per group" to include all tied rows.

Q: What is a materialized view?

A database object that stores the results of a query physically on disk. Unlike a regular view (which executes the query each time), an MV pre-computes and stores results. Can be refreshed on COMMIT, on demand, or on a schedule. Supports QUERY REWRITE for transparent optimization.

Q: What is BULK COLLECT and why use it?

BULK COLLECT fetches multiple rows into PL/SQL collections in a single context switch between PL/SQL and SQL engines. Dramatically reduces the overhead of row-by-row processing. Use with LIMIT to avoid excessive PGA memory usage on large datasets.

Q: Difference between TRUNCATE and DELETE?

DELETE: DML, logged (undo generated), supports WHERE, fires row triggers, rollback-able. TRUNCATE: DDL, minimal logging, no WHERE, no row triggers, auto-commits, resets HWM, much faster for full table clear.

Q: What is partition pruning?

The optimizer's ability to skip partitions not relevant to a query's WHERE clause. E.g., a query filtering on order_date = '2024-01-15' on a range-partitioned table only reads the January 2024 partition, ignoring all others.

Q: What is the difference between an implicit and explicit cursor?

Implicit cursors are automatically created by Oracle for every SELECT INTO, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE. Explicit cursors are declared and managed by the developer using CURSOR, OPEN, FETCH, CLOSE syntax. Explicit cursors offer more control and support BULK COLLECT, parameterization, and FOR UPDATE.

Q: How does the Oracle optimizer decide on a join method?

Based on table statistics (row counts, cardinality, histograms), available indexes, and hints. Nested Loop: efficient when inner table has an index and outer result is small. Hash Join: efficient for large unsorted sets. Sort-Merge: efficient when both sides are already sorted or will be sorted for ORDER BY.

Spring

1. Spring Fundamentals

1.1 IoC Container & Dependency Injection

Definition

Inversion of Control (IoC): The framework creates and manages objects (beans) instead of application code instantiating them directly. Dependency Injection (DI): Dependencies are provided to objects rather than objects creating them.

Bean Lifecycle Diagram

Instantiation
    ↓
Populate Properties (DI)
    ↓
BeanNameAware.setBeanName()
    ↓
BeanFactoryAware.setBeanFactory()
    ↓
ApplicationContextAware.setApplicationContext()
    ↓
BeanPostProcessor.postProcessBeforeInitialization()
    ↓
@PostConstruct / InitializingBean.afterPropertiesSet()
    ↓
@Bean(initMethod)
    ↓
BeanPostProcessor.postProcessAfterInitialization()
    ↓
[BEAN IN USE]
    ↓
@PreDestroy / DisposableBean.destroy()
    ↓
@Bean(destroyMethod)

ApplicationContext Types

// Standalone — Java config
ApplicationContext ctx = new AnnotationConfigApplicationContext(AppConfig.class);

// Web application
ApplicationContext ctx = new AnnotationConfigWebApplicationContext();

// XML-based (legacy)
ApplicationContext ctx = new ClassPathXmlApplicationContext("applicationContext.xml");

// Spring Boot — auto-creates the right type
// SpringApplication.run(App.class, args) → AnnotationConfigServletWebServerApplicationContext

// Accessing beans programmatically
MyService service = ctx.getBean(MyService.class);
MyService service = ctx.getBean("myService", MyService.class);

1.2 Bean Scopes

Scope Description Web Only
singleton One instance per container (default) No
prototype New instance every time requested No
request One per HTTP request Yes
session One per HTTP session Yes
application One per ServletContext Yes
websocket One per WebSocket session Yes 
@Component
@Scope("singleton")          // Default — no need to specify
public class SingletonService { }

@Component
@Scope("prototype")          // New instance each time
public class PrototypeHelper { }

@Component
@Scope(value = WebApplicationContext.SCOPE_REQUEST,
       proxyMode = ScopedProxyMode.TARGET_CLASS)  // Required for injection into singleton
public class RequestScopedBean { }

// Injecting prototype into singleton — use ObjectProvider or Provider
@Service
public class OrderService {
    private final ObjectProvider<OrderProcessor> processorProvider;

    public OrderService(ObjectProvider<OrderProcessor> processorProvider) {
        this.processorProvider = processorProvider;
    }

    public void process() {
        OrderProcessor processor = processorProvider.getObject(); // New instance each call
    }
}

1.3 Environment & Profiles

// @Profile — bean only registered in matching profile
@Configuration
@Profile("production")
public class ProdConfig { }

@Configuration
@Profile({"development", "test"})
public class DevTestConfig { }

@Profile("!production")   // All profiles except production
public class DevBean { }

// Activating profiles
// application.properties
spring.profiles.active=production,monitoring

// JVM argument
// -Dspring.profiles.active=production

// Environment variable
// SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=production

// Programmatic
SpringApplication app = new SpringApplication(MyApp.class);
app.setAdditionalProfiles("production");

// @PropertySource — load additional property files
@Configuration
@PropertySource("classpath:custom.properties")
@PropertySource(value = "file:/etc/app/override.properties", ignoreResourceNotFound = true)
public class AppConfig { }

2. Core Spring Annotations

2.1 Stereotype Annotations

// @Component — generic Spring-managed component
@Component
public class EmailValidator {
    public boolean isValid(String email) { return email.contains("@"); }
}

// @Service — marks business logic layer (no extra behaviour over @Component, but semantic)
@Service
public class UserService {
    private final UserRepository userRepository;
    public UserService(UserRepository userRepository) {  // Constructor injection
        this.userRepository = userRepository;
    }
}

// @Repository — marks data access layer; translates SQL/JPA exceptions to Spring's DataAccessException
@Repository
public class UserRepository {
    // JPA, JDBC operations here
}

// @Controller — marks Spring MVC controller (returns view names)
@Controller
public class HomeController {
    @GetMapping("/")
    public String home(Model model) {
        model.addAttribute("message", "Welcome");
        return "index";   // View name
    }
}

// @RestController — @Controller + @ResponseBody on every method
@RestController
@RequestMapping("/api/v1/users")
public class UserController {
    @GetMapping("/{id}")
    public ResponseEntity<UserDto> getUser(@PathVariable Long id) { ... }
}

2.2 Bean Configuration Annotations

// @Configuration — marks class as source of bean definitions
// Full @Configuration: proxied by CGLIB — @Bean methods return same instance
// Lite @Configuration (@Component with @Bean): not proxied — each @Bean call creates new instance
@Configuration
public class AppConfig {

    // @Bean — method produces a Spring-managed bean
    @Bean
    public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
        return new BCryptPasswordEncoder(12);
    }

    // @Bean with name, init and destroy methods
    @Bean(name = "emailService", initMethod = "init", destroyMethod = "cleanup")
    public EmailService emailService() {
        return new EmailServiceImpl();
    }

    // @Primary — default bean when multiple candidates exist
    @Bean
    @Primary
    public DataSource primaryDataSource() { ... }

    // @Qualifier — distinguish specific bean by name
    @Bean
    @Qualifier("readOnly")
    public DataSource readOnlyDataSource() { ... }

    // @Lazy — delay initialization until first requested
    @Bean
    @Lazy
    public HeavyService heavyService() { ... }

    // @DependsOn — ensure other beans initialized first
    @Bean
    @DependsOn("databaseInitializer")
    public UserService userService() { ... }

    // @Import — import other @Configuration classes
    @Import({SecurityConfig.class, CacheConfig.class})
    public void importConfigs() { }

    // @ImportResource — import XML config files
    @ImportResource("classpath:legacy-beans.xml")
    public void importXml() { }
}

2.3 Dependency Injection

// ✅ Constructor injection (RECOMMENDED — immutable, testable, required deps explicit)
@Service
public class OrderService {
    private final ProductRepository productRepo;
    private final PaymentService paymentService;

    // @Autowired optional when single constructor (Spring 4.3+)
    public OrderService(ProductRepository productRepo, PaymentService paymentService) {
        this.productRepo = productRepo;
        this.paymentService = paymentService;
    }
}

// Setter injection (optional dependencies)
@Service
public class NotificationService {
    private EmailClient emailClient;

    @Autowired(required = false)   // Optional — won't fail if bean missing
    public void setEmailClient(EmailClient emailClient) {
        this.emailClient = emailClient;
    }
}

// Field injection (avoid in production — hard to test, hides dependencies)
@Service
public class LegacyService {
    @Autowired                     // ❌ Avoid — prefer constructor injection
    private UserRepository userRepository;
}

// @Qualifier — resolve ambiguity when multiple beans of same type
@Service
public class DataService {
    private final DataSource dataSource;

    public DataService(@Qualifier("primaryDataSource") DataSource dataSource) {
        this.dataSource = dataSource;
    }
}

// @Primary — set default bean for type
// @Qualifier — override default for specific injection point

// Optional<T> injection
@Service
public class OptionalService {
    @Autowired
    private Optional<CacheManager> cacheManager;  // Won't fail if no CacheManager

    public void cache() {
        cacheManager.ifPresent(cm -> cm.getCache("users"));
    }
}

// Collection injection — all beans of type
@Service
public class PluginRegistry {
    private final List<Plugin> plugins;

    public PluginRegistry(List<Plugin> plugins) {  // Injects ALL Plugin beans
        this.plugins = plugins;
    }
}

// @Inject (JSR-330 — equivalent to @Autowired)
// @Resource (JSR-250 — injects by name first, then by type)

2.4 Lifecycle Annotations

import jakarta.annotation.PostConstruct;   // Spring Boot 3+ (Jakarta)
import jakarta.annotation.PreDestroy;      // Spring Boot 2: javax.annotation.*

@Service
public class CacheWarmupService {

    private final UserRepository userRepository;
    private List<User> cachedUsers;

    public CacheWarmupService(UserRepository userRepository) {
        this.userRepository = userRepository;
    }

    @PostConstruct    // Called after DI complete, before bean published
    public void warmUp() {
        cachedUsers = userRepository.findAll();
        System.out.println("Cache warmed with " + cachedUsers.size() + " users");
    }

    @PreDestroy       // Called before bean destroyed (not for prototype scope)
    public void cleanup() {
        cachedUsers.clear();
        System.out.println("Cache cleared");
    }
}

// Alternatively — implement interfaces
@Component
public class DatabaseInitializer implements InitializingBean, DisposableBean {

    @Override
    public void afterPropertiesSet() throws Exception {
        // Called after properties set — same as @PostConstruct
    }

    @Override
    public void destroy() throws Exception {
        // Called on shutdown — same as @PreDestroy
    }
}

3. Spring Boot Annotations

3.1 Core Boot Annotations

// @SpringBootApplication = @Configuration + @EnableAutoConfiguration + @ComponentScan
@SpringBootApplication
public class MyApplication {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        SpringApplication.run(MyApplication.class, args);
    }
}

// Customize component scan
@SpringBootApplication(scanBasePackages = {"com.company.app", "com.company.shared"})
public class MyApplication { }

// Exclude specific auto-configurations
@SpringBootApplication(exclude = {
    DataSourceAutoConfiguration.class,
    SecurityAutoConfiguration.class
})
public class MyApplication { }

// @ConfigurationProperties — bind properties to typed POJO
@Configuration
@ConfigurationProperties(prefix = "app.mail")
@Validated    // Enable Bean Validation on properties
public class MailProperties {
    @NotBlank
    private String host;
    private int port = 587;
    private boolean auth = true;
    private Map<String, String> templates = new HashMap<>();
    // Getters and setters required (or use @ConstructorBinding in Boot 2.2+)
}

// application.yml
// app:
//   mail:
//     host: smtp.example.com
//     port: 587
//     templates:
//       welcome: "Welcome to our service!"

// @EnableConfigurationProperties — register ConfigurationProperties class
@Configuration
@EnableConfigurationProperties(MailProperties.class)
public class MailConfig { }

// Or use @ConfigurationPropertiesScan on main class (Boot 2.2+)
@SpringBootApplication
@ConfigurationPropertiesScan
public class MyApplication { }

3.2 Conditional Annotations

// @ConditionalOnProperty — register bean only when property set
@Bean
@ConditionalOnProperty(
    prefix = "app.cache",
    name = "enabled",
    havingValue = "true",
    matchIfMissing = false
)
public CacheManager cacheManager() { return new CaffeineCacheManager(); }

// @ConditionalOnMissingBean — only if no bean of this type exists (common in starters)
@Bean
@ConditionalOnMissingBean(PasswordEncoder.class)
public PasswordEncoder defaultPasswordEncoder() {
    return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
}

// @ConditionalOnBean — only if specified bean exists
@Bean
@ConditionalOnBean(DataSource.class)
public JdbcTemplate jdbcTemplate(DataSource dataSource) {
    return new JdbcTemplate(dataSource);
}

// @ConditionalOnClass — only if class on classpath
@Configuration
@ConditionalOnClass(RedisConnectionFactory.class)  // Redis starter on classpath
public class RedisAutoConfig { }

// @ConditionalOnExpression — SpEL expression
@Bean
@ConditionalOnExpression("${app.feature.enabled:false} and '${app.env}'!='test'")
public FeatureService featureService() { ... }

// @Profile — profile-based condition (most common for env-specific beans)
@Bean
@Profile("production")
public MonitoringService monitoringService() { ... }

3.3 Spring Boot Test Annotations

// @SpringBootTest — full application context (integration tests)
@SpringBootTest(
    webEnvironment = SpringBootTest.WebEnvironment.RANDOM_PORT,
    properties = {"app.feature.enabled=true"}
)
class UserServiceIntegrationTest { }

// @MockBean — creates and injects Mockito mock, replaces real bean
@SpringBootTest
class OrderServiceTest {
    @Autowired
    private OrderService orderService;

    @MockBean
    private PaymentGateway paymentGateway;  // Real OrderService, mock PaymentGateway

    @Test
    void shouldProcessOrder() {
        when(paymentGateway.charge(any())).thenReturn(new PaymentResult(true));
        Order result = orderService.process(new Order());
        assertThat(result.isCompleted()).isTrue();
    }
}

// @SpyBean — partial mock (wraps real bean)
@SpyBean
private EmailService emailService;  // Real bean, but can stub specific methods

// Test slices (load minimal context)
@WebMvcTest(UserController.class)    // Only web layer — fast
@DataJpaTest                         // Only JPA layer — uses in-memory h3
@JsonTest                            // JSON serialization only
@RestClientTest(UserClient.class)    // REST client testing

// @TestConfiguration — additional beans for tests only
@TestConfiguration
public class TestConfig {
    @Bean
    public Clock fixedClock() {
        return Clock.fixed(Instant.parse("2024-01-15T00:00:00Z"), ZoneOffset.UTC);
    }
}

4. Spring MVC & REST

4.1 Request Mapping

@RestController
@RequestMapping("/api/v1/users")       // Base path for all methods
public class UserController {

    // @GetMapping = @RequestMapping(method = RequestMethod.GET)
    @GetMapping                         // GET /api/v1/users
    public List<UserDto> getAllUsers(
        @RequestParam(defaultValue = "0") int page,
        @RequestParam(defaultValue = "20") int size,
        @RequestParam(required = false) String sortBy
    ) { ... }

    @GetMapping("/{id}")                // GET /api/v1/users/{id}
    public ResponseEntity<UserDto> getUser(
        @PathVariable Long id
    ) {
        return userService.findById(id)
            .map(ResponseEntity::ok)
            .orElse(ResponseEntity.notFound().build());
    }

    @PostMapping                        // POST /api/v1/users
    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.CREATED)
    public UserDto createUser(
        @RequestBody @Valid CreateUserRequest request  // @Valid triggers Bean Validation
    ) { ... }

    @PutMapping("/{id}")                // PUT /api/v1/users/{id}
    public ResponseEntity<UserDto> updateUser(
        @PathVariable Long id,
        @RequestBody @Valid UpdateUserRequest request
    ) { ... }

    @PatchMapping("/{id}")              // PATCH /api/v1/users/{id}
    public ResponseEntity<UserDto> patchUser(
        @PathVariable Long id,
        @RequestBody Map<String, Object> updates
    ) { ... }

    @DeleteMapping("/{id}")             // DELETE /api/v1/users/{id}
    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)
    public void deleteUser(@PathVariable Long id) { ... }

    // Multiple paths
    @GetMapping({"/me", "/current"})
    public UserDto getCurrentUser() { ... }

    // Headers & Content negotiation
    @GetMapping(produces = MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON_VALUE)
    public List<UserDto> getUsersAsJson() { ... }

    @PostMapping(
        consumes = MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON_VALUE,
        produces = MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON_VALUE
    )
    public UserDto createUser(@RequestBody UserDto user) { ... }
}

4.2 Request Handling Annotations

@RestController
public class ApiController {

    // @RequestParam — query parameter
    @GetMapping("/search")
    public Page<User> search(
        @RequestParam String query,
        @RequestParam(defaultValue = "0") int page,
        @RequestParam(name = "page_size", defaultValue = "20") int pageSize,
        @RequestParam(required = false) List<String> tags  // /search?tags=a&tags=b
    ) { ... }

    // @PathVariable
    @GetMapping("/departments/{deptId}/employees/{empId}")
    public Employee getEmployee(
        @PathVariable Long deptId,
        @PathVariable("empId") Long employeeId
    ) { ... }

    // @RequestHeader
    @PostMapping("/webhook")
    public void handleWebhook(
        @RequestHeader("X-Signature") String signature,
        @RequestHeader(value = "X-Request-ID", required = false) String requestId,
        @RequestBody String payload
    ) { ... }

    // @CookieValue
    @GetMapping("/preferences")
    public Preferences getPrefs(
        @CookieValue(value = "session_id", defaultValue = "anonymous") String sessionId
    ) { ... }

    // @MatrixVariable — /users/42;expand=roles,permissions
    @GetMapping("/users/{id}")
    public UserDto getUser(
        @PathVariable Long id,
        @MatrixVariable(required = false) List<String> expand
    ) { ... }

    // ResponseEntity — full control over status, headers, body
    @GetMapping("/{id}")
    public ResponseEntity<UserDto> getUser(@PathVariable Long id) {
        return userService.findById(id)
            .map(user -> ResponseEntity.ok()
                .header("X-User-Found", "true")
                .body(user))
            .orElse(ResponseEntity.notFound().build());
    }

    // HttpEntity — similar to ResponseEntity but for request too
    @PostMapping("/upload")
    public ResponseEntity<String> upload(HttpEntity<byte[]> requestEntity) {
        HttpHeaders headers = requestEntity.getHeaders();
        byte[] body = requestEntity.getBody();
        return ResponseEntity.ok("Uploaded " + body.length + " bytes");
    }
}

4.3 Exception Handling

// @RestControllerAdvice — global exception handler for all controllers
@RestControllerAdvice
@Slf4j
public class GlobalExceptionHandler {

    // Handle specific exception type
    @ExceptionHandler(ResourceNotFoundException.class)
    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND)
    public ErrorResponse handleNotFound(
            ResourceNotFoundException ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
        log.warn("Resource not found: {}", ex.getMessage());
        return ErrorResponse.of(
            HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND.value(),
            ex.getMessage(),
            request.getRequestURI()
        );
    }

    // Handle validation failures
    @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentNotValidException.class)
    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
    public ValidationErrorResponse handleValidation(MethodArgumentNotValidException ex) {
        Map<String, String> fieldErrors = ex.getBindingResult()
            .getFieldErrors().stream()
            .collect(Collectors.toMap(
                FieldError::getField,
                fe -> Objects.requireNonNullElse(fe.getDefaultMessage(), "Invalid value")
            ));
        return new ValidationErrorResponse("Validation failed", fieldErrors);
    }

    // Handle constraint violations
    @ExceptionHandler(ConstraintViolationException.class)
    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
    public ErrorResponse handleConstraintViolation(ConstraintViolationException ex) {
        String message = ex.getConstraintViolations().stream()
            .map(cv -> cv.getPropertyPath() + ": " + cv.getMessage())
            .collect(Collectors.joining(", "));
        return ErrorResponse.of(400, message, "");
    }

    // Catch-all
    @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class)
    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
    public ErrorResponse handleGeneral(Exception ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
        log.error("Unexpected error at {}", request.getRequestURI(), ex);
        return ErrorResponse.of(500, "Internal server error", request.getRequestURI());
    }
}

// Custom exception
public class ResourceNotFoundException extends RuntimeException {
    public ResourceNotFoundException(String resource, Long id) {
        super(String.format("%s not found with id: %d", resource, id));
    }
}

// Problem Details (RFC 7807 — Spring 6 / Boot 3)
// spring.mvc.problemdetails.enabled=true
// Built-in support — no custom handler needed for standard exceptions

4.4 DispatcherServlet Request Lifecycle

Client Request
    ↓
DispatcherServlet.service()
    ↓
HandlerMapping → finds handler (controller method)
    ↓
HandlerAdapter → executes handler
    ↓
    Handler Interceptors (preHandle)
    ↓
    Controller Method
        - ArgumentResolvers (bind @RequestParam, @PathVariable, @RequestBody)
        - Method execution
        - ReturnValueHandlers (@ResponseBody → MessageConverter → JSON/XML)
    ↓
    Handler Interceptors (postHandle)
    ↓
ViewResolver (if ModelAndView returned, not @ResponseBody)
    ↓
Handler Interceptors (afterCompletion)
    ↓
Response to Client

5. Spring Data JPA

5.1 Entity Mapping

@Entity
@Table(
    name = "users",
    indexes = {
        @Index(name = "idx_user_email", columnList = "email"),
        @Index(name = "idx_user_status_created", columnList = "status, created_at")
    },
    uniqueConstraints = @UniqueConstraint(columnNames = {"email"})
)
@EntityListeners(AuditingEntityListener.class)  // For auditing
@Getter @Setter                                 // Lombok
public class User {

    @Id
    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)  // DB auto-increment
    private Long id;

    // GenerationType options:
    // AUTO     — JPA picks strategy (often TABLE — avoid in production)
    // IDENTITY — DB auto-increment (MySQL, PostgreSQL)
    // SEQUENCE — DB sequence (Oracle, PostgreSQL — better performance)
    // TABLE    — JPA-managed table for IDs (worst performance — avoid)

    @Column(name = "email", nullable = false, unique = true, length = 255)
    private String email;

    @Column(name = "password_hash", nullable = false)
    private String passwordHash;

    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)   // Store as "ACTIVE", not 0/1
    @Column(name = "status")
    private UserStatus status = UserStatus.PENDING;

    @Column(name = "birth_date")
    private LocalDate birthDate;

    @Transient    // Not persisted to DB
    private String displayName;

    @Embedded
    private Address address;    // Embeds Address fields into this table

    // Auditing
    @CreatedDate
    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
    private Instant createdAt;

    @LastModifiedDate
    @Column(name = "updated_at")
    private Instant updatedAt;

    @CreatedBy
    @Column(name = "created_by", updatable = false)
    private String createdBy;

    @LastModifiedBy
    @Column(name = "updated_by")
    private String updatedBy;

    // Optimistic locking
    @Version
    private Long version;
}

@Embeddable
public class Address {
    @Column(name = "street")
    private String street;
    @Column(name = "city")
    private String city;
    @Column(name = "country")
    private String country;
}

// Enable JPA auditing in config
@Configuration
@EnableJpaAuditing(auditorAwareRef = "auditorProvider")
public class JpaConfig {
    @Bean
    public AuditorAware<String> auditorProvider() {
        return () -> Optional.ofNullable(SecurityContextHolder.getContext())
            .map(SecurityContext::getAuthentication)
            .filter(Authentication::isAuthenticated)
            .map(Authentication::getName);
    }
}

5.2 Relationships

@Entity
public class Order {
    @Id @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    private Long id;

    // ManyToOne — many orders belong to one customer
    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)           // ✅ LAZY is the best default
    @JoinColumn(name = "customer_id", nullable = false)
    private Customer customer;

    // OneToMany — one order has many items
    @OneToMany(
        mappedBy = "order",                       // Inverse side — FK is in OrderItem
        cascade = CascadeType.ALL,                // Persist/delete items with order
        orphanRemoval = true,                     // Delete items removed from collection
        fetch = FetchType.LAZY                    // ✅ Always lazy for collections
    )
    @OrderBy("lineNumber ASC")
    private List<OrderItem> items = new ArrayList<>();

    // Helper method to maintain bidirectional consistency
    public void addItem(OrderItem item) {
        items.add(item);
        item.setOrder(this);
    }

    public void removeItem(OrderItem item) {
        items.remove(item);
        item.setOrder(null);
    }
}

@Entity
public class OrderItem {
    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    @JoinColumn(name = "order_id")
    private Order order;
}

// ManyToMany
@Entity
public class Student {
    @ManyToMany(cascade = {CascadeType.PERSIST, CascadeType.MERGE})  // NOT ALL for M2M
    @JoinTable(
        name = "student_courses",
        joinColumns = @JoinColumn(name = "student_id"),
        inverseJoinColumns = @JoinColumn(name = "course_id")
    )
    private Set<Course> courses = new HashSet<>();
}

@Entity
public class Course {
    @ManyToMany(mappedBy = "courses")  // Inverse (non-owning) side
    private Set<Student> students = new HashSet<>();
}

// OneToOne
@Entity
public class UserProfile {
    @OneToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    @JoinColumn(name = "user_id")
    @MapsId                           // Share primary key with User
    private User user;

    @Id
    private Long id;
}

5.3 Repositories

// JpaRepository — extends PagingAndSortingRepository, CrudRepository
@Repository
public interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User, Long> {

    // Derived query methods — Spring Data generates SQL from method name
    Optional<User> findByEmail(String email);
    List<User> findByStatusAndCreatedAtAfter(UserStatus status, Instant since);
    boolean existsByEmail(String email);
    long countByStatus(UserStatus status);
    void deleteByEmail(String email);
    List<User> findTop10ByOrderBySalaryDesc();

    // @Query — JPQL (entity/field names, not table/column names)
    @Query("SELECT u FROM User u WHERE u.email = :email AND u.status = :status")
    Optional<User> findByEmailAndStatus(
        @Param("email") String email,
        @Param("status") UserStatus status
    );

    // Native SQL query
    @Query(
        value = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE YEAR(created_at) = :year",
        nativeQuery = true
    )
    List<User> findByYear(@Param("year") int year);

    // Modifying query
    @Modifying
    @Transactional
    @Query("UPDATE User u SET u.status = :status WHERE u.id = :id")
    int updateStatus(@Param("id") Long id, @Param("status") UserStatus status);

    // Pagination
    Page<User> findByStatus(UserStatus status, Pageable pageable);
    Slice<User> findByCreatedAtAfter(Instant since, Pageable pageable);  // No count query

    // Projections — fetch only needed columns
    List<UserSummary> findAllByStatus(UserStatus status);  // UserSummary is interface or class

    // Specifications (complex dynamic queries)
    List<User> findAll(Specification<User> spec);
}

// Projection interface
public interface UserSummary {
    Long getId();
    String getEmail();
    UserStatus getStatus();
}

// Dynamic queries with Specifications
public class UserSpecifications {
    public static Specification<User> hasStatus(UserStatus status) {
        return (root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get("status"), status);
    }

    public static Specification<User> emailContains(String keyword) {
        return (root, query, cb) -> cb.like(cb.lower(root.get("email")), "%" + keyword.toLowerCase() + "%");
    }
}

// Usage
List<User> activeAdmins = userRepository.findAll(
    UserSpecifications.hasStatus(UserStatus.ACTIVE)
        .and(UserSpecifications.emailContains("admin"))
);

// Pageable usage
Page<User> page = userRepository.findByStatus(
    UserStatus.ACTIVE,
    PageRequest.of(0, 20, Sort.by(Sort.Direction.DESC, "createdAt"))
);

6. Hibernate & ORM

6.1 FetchType & N+1 Problem

// THE N+1 PROBLEM — most common JPA performance issue
// This code issues 1 query for orders + N queries for each customer:
List<Order> orders = orderRepository.findAll();
orders.forEach(o -> System.out.println(o.getCustomer().getName())); // N extra queries!

// ✅ Fix 1: JOIN FETCH in JPQL
@Query("SELECT o FROM Order o JOIN FETCH o.customer WHERE o.status = :status")
List<Order> findWithCustomer(@Param("status") OrderStatus status);

// ✅ Fix 2: EntityGraph — ad-hoc fetch plan
@EntityGraph(attributePaths = {"customer", "items"})
List<Order> findByStatus(OrderStatus status);

// ✅ Fix 3: Named Entity Graph
@Entity
@NamedEntityGraph(
    name = "Order.withCustomer",
    attributeNodes = {
        @NamedAttributeNode("customer"),
        @NamedAttributeNode(value = "items", subgraph = "items.product")
    },
    subgraphs = @NamedSubgraph(name = "items.product", attributeNodes = @NamedAttributeNode("product"))
)
public class Order { }

// Repository usage
@EntityGraph(value = "Order.withCustomer")
List<Order> findAll();

// FetchType guidelines:
// @ManyToOne: LAZY ✅ (default is EAGER — override to LAZY)
// @OneToOne:  LAZY ✅ (default is EAGER — override to LAZY)
// @OneToMany: LAZY ✅ (already default)
// @ManyToMany: LAZY ✅ (already default)
// NEVER use EAGER — causes over-fetching and N+1 in different ways

6.2 CascadeType Reference

CascadeType Description Use Case
PERSIST Save cascades to related Parent → children
MERGE Update cascades Parent → children
REMOVE Delete cascades ⚠️ Only for owned relationships
REFRESH Refresh cascades Usually safe
DETACH Detach cascades Usually safe
ALL All above One-to-many owned only 
// ✅ OneToMany owned: CascadeType.ALL + orphanRemoval
@OneToMany(mappedBy = "order", cascade = CascadeType.ALL, orphanRemoval = true)
private List<OrderItem> items;

// ✅ ManyToMany: only PERSIST + MERGE (never REMOVE — deletes the other entity!)
@ManyToMany(cascade = {CascadeType.PERSIST, CascadeType.MERGE})
private Set<Tag> tags;

// Optimistic Locking — @Version
@Entity
public class Account {
    @Id private Long id;
    private BigDecimal balance;

    @Version   // Auto-incremented on each update; throws OptimisticLockException on conflict
    private Long version;
}

7. Spring Transaction Management

7.1 @Transactional

@Service
public class TransferService {

    // Default: propagation=REQUIRED, isolation=DEFAULT, readOnly=false
    @Transactional
    public void transfer(Long fromId, Long toId, BigDecimal amount) {
        Account from = accountRepository.findById(fromId).orElseThrow();
        Account to   = accountRepository.findById(toId).orElseThrow();
        from.debit(amount);
        to.credit(amount);
        // Transaction commits if no exception; rolls back on RuntimeException
    }

    // Read-only transaction — no flush, potential optimization
    @Transactional(readOnly = true)
    public List<Account> getAccounts() {
        return accountRepository.findAll();
    }

    // Custom rollback rules
    @Transactional(
        rollbackFor = {BusinessException.class, IOException.class},
        noRollbackFor = {ValidationException.class}
    )
    public void riskyOperation() throws IOException { }

    // Timeout
    @Transactional(timeout = 30)   // Seconds — throws TransactionTimedOutException
    public void longRunning() { }
}

7.2 Transaction Propagation

@Service
public class OrderService {

    @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.REQUIRED)  // Default
    // Join existing tx; create new if none
    public void createOrder() {
        inventoryService.reserveItems();  // Joins this transaction
    }

    @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW)
    // Always create new tx; suspend outer if exists
    public void auditLog(String action) {
        // This commits independently (e.g., audit even if outer rolls back)
    }

    @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.NESTED)
    // Savepoint within outer tx; inner rollback doesn't affect outer
    public void nestedOperation() { }

    @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.MANDATORY)
    // Must have existing tx; throws if none
    public void mustBeInTransaction() { }

    @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.NEVER)
    // Must NOT be in tx; throws if one exists
    public void noTransaction() { }

    @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.SUPPORTS)
    // Use existing tx if present; execute non-transactionally if none
    public void optional() { }

    @Transactional(propagation = Propagation.NOT_SUPPORTED)
    // Suspend existing tx; execute non-transactionally
    public void nonTransactional() { }
}

7.3 Transaction Propagation Matrix

┌─────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Propagation     │ Outer TX exists?        Outer TX absent?                │
├─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ REQUIRED        │ Join existing           Create new                      │
│ REQUIRES_NEW    │ Suspend outer, new      Create new                      │
│ NESTED          │ Savepoint in outer      Create new                      │
│ MANDATORY       │ Join existing           Throw exception                 │
│ NEVER           │ Throw exception         Execute without TX               │
│ SUPPORTS        │ Join existing           Execute without TX               │
│ NOT_SUPPORTED   │ Suspend outer           Execute without TX               │
└─────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

7.4 Isolation Levels

@Transactional(isolation = Isolation.READ_COMMITTED)  // Default in most DBs
// Problems:           Non-repeatable reads, Phantom reads (but not dirty reads)

@Transactional(isolation = Isolation.READ_UNCOMMITTED)
// Can read dirty data (uncommitted changes) — rarely used

@Transactional(isolation = Isolation.REPEATABLE_READ)
// Same query returns same data within tx — prevents non-repeatable reads

@Transactional(isolation = Isolation.SERIALIZABLE)
// Full isolation — prevents all anomalies — lowest concurrency, highest safety

Common Mistakes

  • Self-invocation: Calling @Transactional method from within the same class bypasses the proxy — transaction doesn't start
  • Checked exceptions: By default, only RuntimeException and Error trigger rollback — use rollbackFor for checked exceptions
  • Prototype beans: @Transactional doesn't work on prototype-scoped beans

8. Spring Security

8.1 Security Configuration (Spring Boot 3 / Spring Security 6)

@Configuration
@EnableWebSecurity
@EnableMethodSecurity(prePostEnabled = true)   // Enables @PreAuthorize, @PostAuthorize
public class SecurityConfig {

    @Bean
    public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
        return http
            .csrf(csrf -> csrf.disable())       // Disable for stateless REST APIs
            .sessionManagement(session -> session
                .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)  // JWT — no sessions
            )
            .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
                .requestMatchers("/api/auth/**").permitAll()
                .requestMatchers("/api/admin/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
                .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.GET, "/api/products/**").hasAnyRole("USER", "ADMIN")
                .requestMatchers("/actuator/health").permitAll()
                .requestMatchers("/actuator/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
                .anyRequest().authenticated()
            )
            .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class)
            .exceptionHandling(ex -> ex
                .authenticationEntryPoint(customAuthEntryPoint)
                .accessDeniedHandler(customAccessDeniedHandler)
            )
            .build();
    }

    @Bean
    public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration config)
            throws Exception {
        return config.getAuthenticationManager();
    }

    @Bean
    public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
        return new BCryptPasswordEncoder(12);
    }
}

8.2 Security Filter Chain Flow

Request
  ↓
SecurityContextPersistenceFilter
  ↓
LogoutFilter
  ↓
UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter (form login)
  ↓
JwtAuthFilter (custom — before UsernamePassword)
  ↓
BasicAuthenticationFilter
  ↓
RequestCacheAwareFilter
  ↓
SecurityContextHolderAwareRequestFilter
  ↓
AnonymousAuthenticationFilter
  ↓
SessionManagementFilter
  ↓
ExceptionTranslationFilter
  ↓
FilterSecurityInterceptor (authorization decision)
  ↓
Controller

8.3 JWT Implementation

@Component
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class JwtAuthFilter extends OncePerRequestFilter {

    private final JwtService jwtService;
    private final UserDetailsService userDetailsService;

    @Override
    protected void doFilterInternal(HttpServletRequest request,
                                    HttpServletResponse response,
                                    FilterChain filterChain)
            throws ServletException, IOException {

        String authHeader = request.getHeader("Authorization");
        if (authHeader == null || !authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
            filterChain.doFilter(request, response);
            return;
        }

        String jwt = authHeader.substring(7);
        String username = jwtService.extractUsername(jwt);

        if (username != null && SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication() == null) {
            UserDetails userDetails = userDetailsService.loadUserByUsername(username);

            if (jwtService.isTokenValid(jwt, userDetails)) {
                UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken authToken =
                    new UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken(
                        userDetails, null, userDetails.getAuthorities()
                    );
                authToken.setDetails(new WebAuthenticationDetailsSource().buildDetails(request));
                SecurityContextHolder.getContext().setAuthentication(authToken);
            }
        }
        filterChain.doFilter(request, response);
    }
}

// JWT Service
@Service
public class JwtService {
    @Value("${app.jwt.secret}")
    private String secretKey;

    @Value("${app.jwt.expiration-ms}")
    private long expirationMs;

    public String generateToken(UserDetails userDetails) {
        return Jwts.builder()
            .subject(userDetails.getUsername())
            .issuedAt(new Date())
            .expiration(new Date(System.currentTimeMillis() + expirationMs))
            .signWith(getSigningKey(), Jwts.SIG.HS256)
            .compact();
    }

    public boolean isTokenValid(String token, UserDetails userDetails) {
        String username = extractUsername(token);
        return username.equals(userDetails.getUsername()) && !isTokenExpired(token);
    }
}

8.4 Method-Level Security

// Enable in config: @EnableMethodSecurity
@Service
public class UserService {

    @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")
    public List<User> getAllUsers() { ... }

    @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN') or #userId == authentication.principal.id")
    public User getUser(Long userId) { ... }   // Admin or own profile

    @PreAuthorize("hasPermission(#order, 'READ')")
    public Order getOrder(Order order) { ... }  // Custom PermissionEvaluator

    @PostAuthorize("returnObject.ownerId == authentication.principal.id")
    public Document getDocument(Long id) { ... }   // Check after method executes

    @Secured("ROLE_ADMIN")           // Simpler — role check only
    public void adminAction() { ... }

    @RolesAllowed({"ADMIN", "MANAGER"})  // JSR-250
    public void managerAction() { ... }

    // PreFilter — filter input collection before method
    @PreFilter("filterObject.ownerId == authentication.principal.id")
    public void processOrders(List<Order> orders) { ... }

    // PostFilter — filter returned collection
    @PostFilter("filterObject.ownerId == authentication.principal.id")
    public List<Order> getOrders() { ... }
}

9. Spring AOP

9.1 AOP Annotations

@Aspect
@Component
@Slf4j
public class LoggingAspect {

    // Pointcut expressions
    @Pointcut("execution(* com.example.service.*.*(..))")                    // All service methods
    private void serviceMethods() { }

    @Pointcut("@annotation(com.example.annotation.Auditable)")              // Custom annotation
    private void auditableMethods() { }

    @Pointcut("within(com.example.controller..*)")                          // All in package
    private void controllerMethods() { }

    @Pointcut("bean(*Service)")                                              // Beans named *Service
    private void serviceBeans() { }

    // @Before — runs before method
    @Before("serviceMethods()")
    public void logBefore(JoinPoint jp) {
        log.debug("Calling: {}.{}",
            jp.getTarget().getClass().getSimpleName(),
            jp.getSignature().getName());
    }

    // @AfterReturning — after successful return
    @AfterReturning(pointcut = "serviceMethods()", returning = "result")
    public void logAfter(JoinPoint jp, Object result) {
        log.debug("Result: {}", result);
    }

    // @AfterThrowing — after exception thrown
    @AfterThrowing(pointcut = "serviceMethods()", throwing = "ex")
    public void logException(JoinPoint jp, Exception ex) {
        log.error("Exception in {}: {}", jp.getSignature(), ex.getMessage());
    }

    // @After — always runs (like finally)
    @After("serviceMethods()")
    public void logFinally(JoinPoint jp) {
        log.debug("Completed: {}", jp.getSignature());
    }

    // @Around — most powerful — controls execution
    @Around("@annotation(com.example.annotation.Timed)")
    public Object measureTime(ProceedingJoinPoint pjp) throws Throwable {
        long start = System.currentTimeMillis();
        try {
            Object result = pjp.proceed();    // Actually call the method
            long elapsed = System.currentTimeMillis() - start;
            log.info("{} took {}ms", pjp.getSignature(), elapsed);
            return result;
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Exception after {}ms in {}", System.currentTimeMillis() - start, pjp.getSignature());
            throw e;
        }
    }

    // @Around with annotation access
    @Around("@annotation(retryable)")
    public Object retry(ProceedingJoinPoint pjp, Retryable retryable) throws Throwable {
        int maxAttempts = retryable.maxAttempts();
        for (int i = 0; i < maxAttempts; i++) {
            try {
                return pjp.proceed();
            } catch (Exception e) {
                if (i == maxAttempts - 1) throw e;
                Thread.sleep(retryable.backoffMs());
            }
        }
        return null;
    }
}

// Custom annotation
@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
@Target(ElementType.METHOD)
public @interface Retryable {
    int maxAttempts() default 3;
    long backoffMs() default 1000;
}

Common Mistakes

  • AOP only works on Spring beans (not new instances)
  • Self-invocation doesn't trigger aspects (same as @Transactional)
  • @Around must call pjp.proceed() or the actual method is never called

10. Spring Events

// Define custom event
public class UserRegisteredEvent extends ApplicationEvent {
    private final User user;

    public UserRegisteredEvent(Object source, User user) {
        super(source);
        this.user = user;
    }
    public User getUser() { return user; }
}

// Publish event
@Service
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class UserService {
    private final ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher;

    public User register(CreateUserRequest request) {
        User user = createUser(request);
        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new UserRegisteredEvent(this, user));
        // Or simpler (Spring 4.2+):
        eventPublisher.publishEvent(user);  // Any object — use @EventListener with payload type
        return user;
    }
}

// Listen to event
@Component
@Slf4j
public class UserEventHandler {

    @EventListener
    public void onUserRegistered(UserRegisteredEvent event) {
        log.info("User registered: {}", event.getUser().getEmail());
        sendWelcomeEmail(event.getUser());
    }

    // Conditional event listener
    @EventListener(condition = "#event.user.email.endsWith('@premium.com')")
    public void onPremiumUserRegistered(UserRegisteredEvent event) {
        activatePremiumFeatures(event.getUser());
    }

    // Transaction-bound event — fires after TX commits
    @TransactionalEventListener(phase = TransactionPhase.AFTER_COMMIT)
    public void afterUserSaved(UserRegisteredEvent event) {
        // Only fires if the outer transaction committed successfully
        notifyExternalSystem(event.getUser());
    }

    // Async event
    @EventListener
    @Async
    public void asyncHandle(UserRegisteredEvent event) {
        // Runs in separate thread — requires @EnableAsync
        generateUserReport(event.getUser());
    }
}

11. Spring Scheduling & Async

11.1 Scheduling

@SpringBootApplication
@EnableScheduling
@EnableAsync
public class MyApplication { }

@Component
@Slf4j
public class ScheduledTasks {

    // Fixed rate — every N ms regardless of execution time (overlapping possible)
    @Scheduled(fixedRate = 30000)           // Every 30 seconds
    public void heartbeat() {
        log.debug("Heartbeat");
    }

    // Fixed delay — N ms AFTER previous execution completes
    @Scheduled(fixedDelay = 60000)          // 60s after previous finishes
    public void cleanupTemp() {
        tempFileService.cleanup();
    }

    // Initial delay + fixed rate
    @Scheduled(initialDelay = 10000, fixedRate = 60000)  // Start after 10s
    public void cachRefresh() { }

    // Cron expression
    @Scheduled(cron = "0 0 2 * * ?")        // Every day at 2:00 AM
    public void nightlyReport() { }

    @Scheduled(cron = "0 0 9 ? * MON")      // Every Monday at 9:00 AM
    public void weeklyDigest() { }

    @Scheduled(cron = "0 0/15 * * * ?")     // Every 15 minutes
    public void frequentTask() { }

    // From property
    @Scheduled(cron = "${app.scheduler.report-cron:0 0 8 * * ?}")
    public void configuredSchedule() { }
}

// Cron format: Seconds Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week [Year]
// 0 30 10-13 ? * WED,FRI  — 10:30, 11:30, 12:30, 13:30 on Wed and Fri
// 0 0/5 14 * * ?           — Every 5 minutes from 2pm to 2:55pm, every day
// 0 15 10 15 * ?           — 10:15am on the 15th of every month
// 0 0 12 L * ?             — 12pm on last day of every month

11.2 @Async

@Service
public class AsyncService {

    @Async                              // Default thread pool (SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor)
    public CompletableFuture<String> asyncOperation(String input) {
        // Runs in separate thread
        return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(process(input));
    }

    @Async("emailTaskExecutor")         // Named thread pool
    public void sendEmail(String to, String body) {
        emailClient.send(to, body);
    }
}

// Configure thread pool
@Configuration
@EnableAsync
public class AsyncConfig implements AsyncConfigurer {

    @Bean(name = "emailTaskExecutor")
    public Executor emailTaskExecutor() {
        ThreadPoolTaskExecutor executor = new ThreadPoolTaskExecutor();
        executor.setCorePoolSize(5);
        executor.setMaxPoolSize(20);
        executor.setQueueCapacity(500);
        executor.setThreadNamePrefix("email-");
        executor.setRejectedExecutionHandler(new ThreadPoolExecutor.CallerRunsPolicy());
        executor.initialize();
        return executor;
    }

    @Override
    public Executor getAsyncExecutor() {
        return emailTaskExecutor();     // Default for @Async without name
    }

    @Override
    public AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler getAsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler() {
        return (ex, method, params) ->
            log.error("Async exception in {}: {}", method.getName(), ex.getMessage(), ex);
    }
}

12. Reactive Spring — WebFlux

12.1 Mono & Flux

import reactor.core.publisher.Mono;
import reactor.core.publisher.Flux;

// Mono — 0 or 1 element
Mono<String> mono = Mono.just("Hello");
Mono<String> empty = Mono.empty();
Mono<String> error = Mono.error(new RuntimeException("failed"));
Mono<String> deferred = Mono.defer(() -> Mono.just(expensiveCompute()));

// Flux — 0 to N elements
Flux<Integer> flux = Flux.just(1, 2, 3, 4, 5);
Flux<Integer> range = Flux.range(1, 10);
Flux<String> fromList = Flux.fromIterable(List.of("a", "b", "c"));

// Operators — lazy, nothing executes until subscribe()
Flux.range(1, 100)
    .filter(n -> n % 2 == 0)
    .map(n -> n * n)
    .take(5)
    .subscribe(System.out::println);

// Error handling
mono.onErrorReturn("default")
    .onErrorResume(ex -> Mono.just("fallback"))
    .onErrorMap(ex -> new BusinessException("Wrapped", ex));

// Combining
Mono.zip(fetchUser(id), fetchProfile(id))
    .map(tuple -> new UserWithProfile(tuple.getT1(), tuple.getT2()));

Flux.merge(flux1, flux2);         // Merge (interleave as they arrive)
Flux.concat(flux1, flux2);        // Concat (flux1 complete, then flux2)
Flux.zip(flux1, flux2);           // Zip corresponding elements

// flatMap vs concatMap vs switchMap
flux.flatMap(item -> fetchAsync(item))    // Concurrent — max concurrency configurable
flux.concatMap(item -> fetchAsync(item))  // Sequential — one at a time
flux.switchMap(item -> fetchAsync(item))  // Cancel previous, use latest

12.2 WebFlux Controllers

// Annotation-based (same annotations as MVC — recommended for most cases)
@RestController
@RequestMapping("/api/v1/users")
public class UserController {

    private final ReactiveUserService userService;

    @GetMapping
    public Flux<UserDto> getAllUsers() {
        return userService.findAll();   // Returns Flux — server-sent events possible
    }

    @GetMapping("/{id}")
    public Mono<ResponseEntity<UserDto>> getUser(@PathVariable Long id) {
        return userService.findById(id)
            .map(ResponseEntity::ok)
            .defaultIfEmpty(ResponseEntity.notFound().build());
    }

    @PostMapping
    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.CREATED)
    public Mono<UserDto> createUser(@RequestBody @Valid Mono<CreateUserRequest> request) {
        return request.flatMap(userService::create);
    }

    // Server-Sent Events (SSE)
    @GetMapping(value = "/stream", produces = MediaType.TEXT_EVENT_STREAM_VALUE)
    public Flux<UserDto> streamUsers() {
        return userService.findAll().delayElements(Duration.ofSeconds(1));
    }
}

// Functional endpoints (router + handler)
@Configuration
public class UserRouter {

    @Bean
    public RouterFunction<ServerResponse> userRoutes(UserHandler handler) {
        return RouterFunctions.route()
            .GET("/api/users", handler::getAllUsers)
            .GET("/api/users/{id}", handler::getUser)
            .POST("/api/users", handler::createUser)
            .build();
    }
}

@Component
public class UserHandler {
    private final ReactiveUserService userService;

    public Mono<ServerResponse> getAllUsers(ServerRequest request) {
        return ServerResponse.ok()
            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)
            .body(userService.findAll(), UserDto.class);
    }

    public Mono<ServerResponse> getUser(ServerRequest request) {
        Long id = Long.valueOf(request.pathVariable("id"));
        return userService.findById(id)
            .flatMap(user -> ServerResponse.ok().bodyValue(user))
            .switchIfEmpty(ServerResponse.notFound().build());
    }
}

12.3 WebClient (Reactive HTTP Client)

// WebClient replaces RestTemplate in reactive apps
@Bean
public WebClient webClient(WebClient.Builder builder) {
    return builder
        .baseUrl("https://api.example.com")
        .defaultHeader(HttpHeaders.CONTENT_TYPE, MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON_VALUE)
        .codecs(configurer -> configurer.defaultCodecs().maxInMemorySize(10 * 1024 * 1024))
        .filter(ExchangeFilterFunction.ofRequestProcessor(request -> {
            log.debug("Request: {} {}", request.method(), request.url());
            return Mono.just(request);
        }))
        .build();
}

@Service
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class UserApiClient {
    private final WebClient webClient;

    public Mono<UserDto> fetchUser(Long id) {
        return webClient.get()
            .uri("/users/{id}", id)
            .retrieve()
            .onStatus(HttpStatusCode::is4xxClientError,
                response -> response.bodyToMono(ErrorResponse.class)
                    .map(err -> new UserNotFoundException(err.getMessage())))
            .onStatus(HttpStatusCode::is5xxServerError,
                response -> Mono.error(new ServiceUnavailableException()))
            .bodyToMono(UserDto.class)
            .timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(5))
            .retryWhen(Retry.backoff(3, Duration.ofMillis(500)));
    }

    public Flux<UserDto> fetchAllUsers() {
        return webClient.get()
            .uri("/users")
            .retrieve()
            .bodyToFlux(UserDto.class);
    }
}

12.4 Reactive vs Servlet Comparison

Aspect Servlet (MVC) Reactive (WebFlux)
Thread model Thread per request Event loop (few threads)
Concurrency Blocking I/O Non-blocking I/O
Return types T, ResponseEntity<T> Mono<T>, Flux<T>
Database JPA (blocking) R2DBC (reactive)
HTTP client RestTemplate WebClient
Best for CRUD, OLTP Streaming, high concurrency I/O
Learning curve Lower Higher
Debugging Easier Harder (async stack traces)

13. Spring Cloud

13.1 Service Discovery — Eureka

// Server
@SpringBootApplication
@EnableEurekaServer
public class EurekaServerApplication { }

// Client
@SpringBootApplication
@EnableEurekaClient  // Or just spring-cloud-starter-netflix-eureka-client on classpath
public class OrderServiceApplication { }

// application.yml — client
// eureka:
//   client:
//     service-url:
//       defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/
// spring:
//   application:
//     name: order-service

13.2 OpenFeign — Declarative REST Client

// Enable
@SpringBootApplication
@EnableFeignClients
public class MyApplication { }

// Feign client — like a Spring MVC controller but for client-side
@FeignClient(
    name = "user-service",           // Service discovery name
    url = "${app.user-service.url}", // Direct URL (overrides discovery)
    fallback = UserClientFallback.class,  // Resilience4j circuit breaker fallback
    configuration = FeignConfig.class
)
public interface UserClient {

    @GetMapping("/api/users/{id}")
    UserDto getUser(@PathVariable Long id);

    @GetMapping("/api/users")
    Page<UserDto> getUsers(@RequestParam int page, @RequestParam int size);

    @PostMapping("/api/users")
    UserDto createUser(@RequestBody CreateUserRequest request);
}

// Fallback
@Component
public class UserClientFallback implements UserClient {
    @Override
    public UserDto getUser(Long id) {
        return UserDto.unknown(id);   // Graceful degradation
    }
}

13.3 Circuit Breaker — Resilience4j

@Service
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class PaymentService {

    private final CircuitBreakerRegistry circuitBreakerRegistry;

    @CircuitBreaker(name = "payment", fallbackMethod = "paymentFallback")
    @Retry(name = "payment", fallbackMethod = "paymentFallback")
    @TimeLimiter(name = "payment")
    @Bulkhead(name = "payment")
    public CompletableFuture<PaymentResult> processPayment(PaymentRequest request) {
        return CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(() -> externalPaymentGateway.process(request));
    }

    public CompletableFuture<PaymentResult> paymentFallback(
            PaymentRequest request, Exception ex) {
        log.warn("Payment circuit open, using fallback: {}", ex.getMessage());
        return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(PaymentResult.pending(request.getId()));
    }
}

// application.yml
// resilience4j:
//   circuit-breaker:
//     instances:
//       payment:
//         register-health-indicator: true
//         sliding-window-size: 10
//         minimum-number-of-calls: 5
//         failure-rate-threshold: 50
//         wait-duration-in-open-state: 30s
//         permitted-number-of-calls-in-half-open-state: 3
//   retry:
//     instances:
//       payment:
//         max-attempts: 3
//         wait-duration: 500ms
//         retry-exceptions:
//           - java.net.ConnectException
//           - java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException

13.4 Spring Cloud Config

// Config Server
@SpringBootApplication
@EnableConfigServer
public class ConfigServerApplication { }

// Config Client — application.yml (or bootstrap.yml in older versions)
// spring:
//   config:
//     import: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888"
//   application:
//     name: order-service
//   profiles:
//     active: production

// Refresh config at runtime
@RestController
@RefreshScope    // Re-creates bean when /actuator/refresh called
public class ConfigController {
    @Value("${app.feature.flag}")
    private boolean featureFlag;
}

14. Messaging — Kafka & RabbitMQ

14.1 Apache Kafka

// Producer
@Service
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class OrderEventProducer {
    private final KafkaTemplate<String, OrderEvent> kafkaTemplate;

    public void publishOrderCreated(Order order) {
        OrderEvent event = OrderEvent.created(order);
        kafkaTemplate.send("order-events", order.getId().toString(), event)
            .whenComplete((result, ex) -> {
                if (ex != null) {
                    log.error("Failed to send event: {}", ex.getMessage());
                } else {
                    log.debug("Event sent to partition {} offset {}",
                        result.getRecordMetadata().partition(),
                        result.getRecordMetadata().offset());
                }
            });
    }
}

// Consumer
@Component
@Slf4j
public class OrderEventConsumer {

    @KafkaListener(
        topics = "order-events",
        groupId = "inventory-service",
        concurrency = "3"              // 3 consumer threads
    )
    public void handleOrderEvent(
            @Payload OrderEvent event,
            @Header(KafkaHeaders.RECEIVED_TOPIC) String topic,
            @Header(KafkaHeaders.RECEIVED_PARTITION) int partition,
            @Header(KafkaHeaders.OFFSET) long offset,
            Acknowledgment ack) {
        log.info("Received event {} from {}:{}", event, topic, partition);
        try {
            inventoryService.process(event);
            ack.acknowledge();         // Manual acknowledgment
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Failed to process, will retry", e);
            // Don't ack — will be redelivered (or use DLT)
        }
    }

    // Batch listener
    @KafkaListener(topics = "bulk-events", batch = "true")
    public void handleBatch(List<OrderEvent> events) {
        log.info("Processing batch of {}", events.size());
    }
}

// application.yml
// spring:
//   kafka:
//     bootstrap-servers: localhost:9092
//     producer:
//       key-serializer: org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer
//       value-serializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonSerializer
//       acks: all
//       retries: 3
//     consumer:
//       group-id: my-service
//       auto-offset-reset: earliest
//       key-deserializer: org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
//       value-deserializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonDeserializer
//       enable-auto-commit: false
//     listener:
//       ack-mode: manual

14.2 RabbitMQ

// Configuration
@Configuration
public class RabbitConfig {

    public static final String EXCHANGE  = "order.exchange";
    public static final String QUEUE     = "order.created.queue";
    public static final String DLQ       = "order.created.dlq";
    public static final String ROUTING   = "order.created";

    @Bean
    public TopicExchange orderExchange() {
        return new TopicExchange(EXCHANGE, true, false);
    }

    @Bean
    public Queue orderQueue() {
        return QueueBuilder.durable(QUEUE)
            .withArgument("x-dead-letter-exchange", "")
            .withArgument("x-dead-letter-routing-key", DLQ)
            .withArgument("x-message-ttl", 60000)
            .build();
    }

    @Bean
    public Binding orderBinding() {
        return BindingBuilder.bind(orderQueue()).to(orderExchange()).with(ROUTING);
    }
}

// Publisher
@Service
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class OrderEventPublisher {
    private final RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate;

    public void publishOrderCreated(Order order) {
        rabbitTemplate.convertAndSend(
            RabbitConfig.EXCHANGE,
            RabbitConfig.ROUTING,
            new OrderCreatedEvent(order)
        );
    }
}

// Consumer
@Component
public class OrderConsumer {

    @RabbitListener(queues = RabbitConfig.QUEUE)
    public void handleOrderCreated(
            OrderCreatedEvent event,
            @Header(AmqpHeaders.DELIVERY_TAG) long deliveryTag,
            Channel channel) throws IOException {
        try {
            processOrder(event);
            channel.basicAck(deliveryTag, false);     // Acknowledge
        } catch (Exception e) {
            channel.basicNack(deliveryTag, false, false);  // Send to DLQ
        }
    }
}

15. Caching

// Enable caching
@SpringBootApplication
@EnableCaching
public class MyApplication { }

// CacheManager configuration
@Configuration
public class CacheConfig {

    @Bean
    public CacheManager cacheManager() {
        CaffeineCacheManager manager = new CaffeineCacheManager();
        manager.setCaffeine(Caffeine.newBuilder()
            .maximumSize(1000)
            .expireAfterWrite(Duration.ofMinutes(10))
            .recordStats()
        );
        manager.setCacheNames(List.of("users", "products", "categories"));
        return manager;
    }
}

@Service
public class UserService {

    // @Cacheable — cache method result by key
    @Cacheable(value = "users", key = "#id")
    public UserDto getUser(Long id) {
        return userRepository.findById(id)
            .map(userMapper::toDto)
            .orElseThrow(() -> new ResourceNotFoundException("User", id));
    }

    // @Cacheable with condition — only cache if result matches
    @Cacheable(value = "users", key = "#email", unless = "#result == null")
    public UserDto findByEmail(String email) { ... }

    // @CachePut — always execute method AND update cache
    @CachePut(value = "users", key = "#result.id")
    public UserDto updateUser(Long id, UpdateUserRequest request) {
        User user = userRepository.findById(id).orElseThrow();
        userMapper.update(user, request);
        return userMapper.toDto(userRepository.save(user));
    }

    // @CacheEvict — remove from cache
    @CacheEvict(value = "users", key = "#id")
    public void deleteUser(Long id) {
        userRepository.deleteById(id);
    }

    // @CacheEvict all entries in cache
    @CacheEvict(value = "users", allEntries = true)
    public void clearUserCache() { }

    // @Caching — combine multiple cache annotations
    @Caching(evict = {
        @CacheEvict(value = "users", key = "#id"),
        @CacheEvict(value = "user-summary", key = "#id")
    })
    public void evictAll(Long id) { }
}

16. Validation

// Entity/DTO validation
public class CreateUserRequest {

    @NotBlank(message = "Name is required")
    @Size(min = 2, max = 100, message = "Name must be between 2 and 100 characters")
    private String name;

    @NotBlank
    @Email(message = "Must be a valid email address")
    private String email;

    @NotNull
    @Min(value = 18, message = "Must be at least 18 years old")
    @Max(value = 120)
    private Integer age;

    @NotNull
    @Past(message = "Birth date must be in the past")
    private LocalDate birthDate;

    @NotNull
    @Future
    private LocalDate expiryDate;

    @Pattern(regexp = "^\\+?[1-9]\\d{1,14}$", message = "Invalid phone number")
    private String phone;

    @NotEmpty
    @Valid                           // Validate nested object
    private Address address;

    @NotNull
    @DecimalMin("0.00")
    @DecimalMax("999999.99")
    private BigDecimal salary;
}

// Custom constraint annotation
@Target({ElementType.FIELD, ElementType.PARAMETER})
@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
@Constraint(validatedBy = UniqueEmailValidator.class)
public @interface UniqueEmail {
    String message() default "Email already in use";
    Class<?>[] groups() default {};
    Class<? extends Payload>[] payload() default {};
}

// Validator implementation
@Component
public class UniqueEmailValidator implements ConstraintValidator<UniqueEmail, String> {
    private final UserRepository userRepository;

    @Override
    public boolean isValid(String email, ConstraintValidatorContext context) {
        if (email == null) return true;  // Null handling is @NotNull's job
        return !userRepository.existsByEmail(email);
    }
}

// @Validated on class — enables validation on method parameters and return values
@Service
@Validated
public class ProductService {
    public Product createProduct(@Valid @NotNull CreateProductRequest request) { ... }
    public @Valid Product getProduct(@Positive Long id) { ... }
}

17. Spring Profiles & Configuration

17.1 application.yml Structure

# Base configuration (all profiles)
spring:
  application:
    name: order-service

  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:5432}/${DB_NAME:orders}
    username: ${DB_USER:orders_user}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 20
      minimum-idle: 5
      connection-timeout: 30000
      idle-timeout: 600000
      max-lifetime: 1800000
      pool-name: orders-pool

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate          # production: validate; dev: create-drop
    show-sql: false               # Never true in production
    properties:
      hibernate:
        format_sql: false
        jdbc:
          batch_size: 25
          fetch_size: 100
        order_inserts: true
        order_updates: true

  cache:
    type: caffeine

server:
  port: 8080
  shutdown: graceful              # Wait for active requests to finish
  tomcat:
    threads:
      max: 200
      min-spare: 20
    max-connections: 10000
    accept-count: 100

management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics,prometheus
  health:
    show-details: when-authorized

app:
  jwt:
    secret: ${JWT_SECRET}
    expiration-ms: 86400000       # 24 hours

logging:
  level:
    root: INFO
    com.example: DEBUG
  pattern:
    console: "%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss} [%thread] %-5level %logger{36} - %msg%n"

---
# Profile-specific (--- separator)
spring:
  config:
    activate:
      on-profile: development

  datasource:
    url: jdbc:h3:mem:testdb
    driver-class-name: org.h3.Driver

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: create-drop
    show-sql: true

  h3:
    console:
      enabled: true

logging:
  level:
    com.example: DEBUG
    org.hibernate.SQL: DEBUG
    org.hibernate.type.descriptor.sql: TRACE

---
spring:
  config:
    activate:
      on-profile: production

  datasource:
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 50

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate

logging:
  level:
    root: WARN
    com.example: INFO

18. Testing

18.1 Test Slices

// @WebMvcTest — only web layer, controllers, filters, security
@WebMvcTest(UserController.class)
class UserControllerTest {

    @Autowired
    private MockMvc mockMvc;

    @MockBean
    private UserService userService;        // Mock service dependency

    @Autowired
    private ObjectMapper objectMapper;

    @Test
    void shouldReturnUser() throws Exception {
        UserDto user = new UserDto(1L, "alice@example.com", "Alice");
        when(userService.findById(1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(user));

        mockMvc.perform(get("/api/v1/users/1")
                .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON))
            .andExpect(status().isOk())
            .andExpect(jsonPath("$.id").value(1))
            .andExpect(jsonPath("$.email").value("alice@example.com"))
            .andDo(print());
    }

    @Test
    void shouldReturn404WhenUserNotFound() throws Exception {
        when(userService.findById(999L)).thenReturn(Optional.empty());

        mockMvc.perform(get("/api/v1/users/999"))
            .andExpect(status().isNotFound());
    }

    @Test
    void shouldCreateUser() throws Exception {
        CreateUserRequest request = new CreateUserRequest("Alice", "alice@example.com", 25);
        UserDto created = new UserDto(1L, "alice@example.com", "Alice");
        when(userService.create(any())).thenReturn(created);

        mockMvc.perform(post("/api/v1/users")
                .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)
                .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(request)))
            .andExpect(status().isCreated())
            .andExpect(jsonPath("$.id").exists());
    }
}

// @DataJpaTest — only JPA layer, in-memory h3 by default
@DataJpaTest
@AutoConfigureTestDatabase(replace = AutoConfigureTestDatabase.Replace.NONE)  // Use real DB
class UserRepositoryTest {

    @Autowired
    private UserRepository userRepository;

    @Autowired
    private TestEntityManager entityManager;

    @Test
    void shouldFindByEmail() {
        User user = entityManager.persistAndFlush(new User("alice@example.com"));
        Optional<User> found = userRepository.findByEmail("alice@example.com");
        assertThat(found).isPresent();
        assertThat(found.get().getEmail()).isEqualTo("alice@example.com");
    }
}

// Integration test
@SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = SpringBootTest.WebEnvironment.RANDOM_PORT)
@Testcontainers
class OrderIntegrationTest {

    @Container
    static PostgreSQLContainer<?> postgres = new PostgreSQLContainer<>("postgres:15")
        .withDatabaseName("test_orders")
        .withUsername("test")
        .withPassword("test");

    @DynamicPropertySource
    static void configureProperties(DynamicPropertyRegistry registry) {
        registry.add("spring.datasource.url", postgres::getJdbcUrl);
        registry.add("spring.datasource.username", postgres::getUsername);
        registry.add("spring.datasource.password", postgres::getPassword);
    }

    @Autowired
    private TestRestTemplate restTemplate;

    @Test
    void shouldCreateOrder() {
        ResponseEntity<OrderDto> response = restTemplate.postForEntity(
            "/api/v1/orders", new CreateOrderRequest(...), OrderDto.class
        );
        assertThat(response.getStatusCode()).isEqualTo(HttpStatus.CREATED);
    }
}

19. Native Images & AOT

// Spring Boot 3 + GraalVM Native Image
// Maven: ./mvnw -Pnative native:compile
